commits: 531b32f docs(0.8.0): Slice 6 — FTS5 tokenizer latency experiment (B2) findings + HITL recommendation

measurement_context.box: x86_64, 24 logical cores, Linux 6.17 (NOT the aarch64 box the Slice-40 B3 note implied; closer to the canonical x86_64 tier-1 runner)
measurement_context.isolation: each perf run executed alone; no concurrent cargo test (per Slice-40 harness caveat)
measurement_context.sqlite: bundled vendored amalgamation via rusqlite 0.31 features=[bundled]; porter/unicode61/ascii are built-ins
measurement_context.instruments: real ac_012 (authoritative gate-level, coarse power-of-two percentile_ceil) + a throwaway SLICE6_EXPERIMENT-gated FTS-path harness (faithful SQL + byte-identical corpus, finer sorted percentiles) + one temporary-and-reverted engine-level A/B edit
measurement_context.absolute_caveat: absolute ms are box-specific; the A/B and tier conclusions are what transfer, not the magnitudes

q1_tier_result.question: at which tier does the regression breach a BINDING budget?

real_ac012_release_isolated.10k: p50 < 1ms, p99 4ms — PASS 20/150 by a wide margin (BINDING tier per AC-013/AC-019 precedent)

q1_tier_result.answer: Breaches ONLY at the unconditional 100k tier, and there only on p50 by ~1ms. ac_012 was simply never tiered to match AC-013/AC-019 (which gate only at AC013_GATE_N=10_000). This is the cleanest resolution surfaced to HITL.
q1_tier_result.note: Slice-40 reported 49.9/315 @100k; not reproduced on this idle x86_64 box (got 21/99). The direction reproduces; the magnitude is hardware/load sensitive (same class as B3).

q2_config_table_summary.latency_sweep_release: All 6 configs (porter unicode61 remove_diacritics 2 [CURRENT] / unicode61 remove_diacritics 2 / porter unicode61 / unicode61 [≈v0.7.2] / porter ascii / ascii) are within noise of each other at BOTH tiers. 10k: p50 ~0.32ms, p99 ~0.9ms, avg_rows 320. 100k: p50 ~3.93-4.05ms, p99 ~15.9-16.1ms, avg_rows 3212, pages 18047. Identical avg_rows + pages because porter is a no-op on the digit-bearing synthetic AC-012 vocab.
q2_config_table_summary.engine_level_ab_100k: CURRENT porter config p50 21ms / p99 99ms vs v0.7.2 'unicode61' p50 20ms / p99 99ms — IDENTICAL within noise at the full engine level. Tokenizer is NOT a latency lever; refutes the Slice-40 tokenizer root-cause attribution.
q2_config_table_summary.quality_proxy: 8 morphology/diacritic queries [run running ran optimize cafe naive resume connect]: CURRENT=8/8; porter unicode61=8/8; unicode61(+rd2)=5/8 (loses stemming run/optimize/connect); unicode61[≈v0.7.2]=5/8; porter ascii=5/8 (loses diacritics cafe/naive/resume); ascii=2/8. Porter buys morphological recall; diacritic folding (already in unicode61 default rd=1) buys accent-insensitivity. So the Slice-5 INCREMENTAL FTS-quality gain over v0.7.2 default unicode61 is specifically porter stemming.

q3_cost_attribution: Cost tracks RESULT-SET SIZE, not tokenization. @100k current tokenizer: 1-token query matches ~3212 rows -> p50 3.93ms; 2-token AND -> ~2 rows -> 0.25ms; 3-token -> ~0 rows -> 0.19ms. The engine FTS SQL is UNBOUNDED (no LIMIT, ORDER BY write_cursor) so it scans the posting list, bm25-scores, and materializes EVERY matched row. Query-time porter stemming is one stem/token = negligible (free at engine level per Q2b). Only real query-path lever = bounding matched-row materialization (dormant FATHOMDB_PERF_SEARCH_LIMIT at lib.rs:3912) — a recall-affecting semantic change, OUT OF SCOPE. Secondary: engine adds ~17ms p50 @100k over raw FTS (reader-pool + dedup-on-body + SearchHit materialization), also growing with matched rows, but tokenizer-independent.

q4_source_accessibility.bundled: SQLite/FTS5 = bundled vendored amalgamation (rusqlite 0.31 features=[bundled]); you do NOT patch SQLite source.
q4_source_accessibility.a_swap_builtins: Trivial — the migration tokenize= string (one line + lib.rs mirror; drop+recreate+reproject like step-11). NO latency motivation (config latency-neutral); pure quality knob.
q4_source_accessibility.b_custom_fts5_tokenizer: Feasible but awkward — rusqlite does not expose fts5_api ergonomically; needs unsafe FFI to the bundled FTS5 C API (or a C shim) + lifetime/registration care. Medium-high cost. NOT motivated for 0.8.0.
q4_source_accessibility.c_embedder: candle/bge-small (fathomdb-embedder) in-tree but IRRELEVANT to this path — ac_012 opens without an embedder; the FTS5 text path never calls the embedder. Embedder bears only on the vector/AC-013 latency (separate, already tiered).

recommendation: Option (i): TIER AC-012 to match AC-013/AC-019 (10k binding, 100k/1M tracked-not-gated), via the existing AC-072/AC-073 precedent (ADR-0.7.0-text-query-latency-gates-revised). Grounds (all measured): (1) cost is O(N) corpus-scaling not the tokenizer; (2) direct HITL-locked precedent exists (AC-072 superseded AC-013, AC-073 superseded AC-019); (3) passes the binding 10k tier by a wide margin; (4) the only thing Slice-5 changed (tokenizer) is exonerated, so 'remediate the tokenizer' cannot fix the 100k number and would degrade FTS quality — keep the G1 porter stemming. Mechanically (Slice 7, NOT done here): mint AC-076 mirroring ac_013's AC013_GATE_N branch (assert only at AC012_GATE_N=10_000; report 100k/1M); gated slice (acceptance.md locked).

hitl_fork.i_tier_ac012: RECOMMENDED — precedented, latency-honest, preserves G1 quality, passes binding tier
hitl_fork.ii_lighten_tokenizer: REJECT — ~0ms latency gain (Q2a/Q2b) and -3/8 FTS recall (Q2c); wrong lever
hitl_fork.iii_custom_tokenizer: NOT for 0.8.0 — medium-high FFI cost, no latency motivation (Q4)
hitl_fork.iv_reratify_100k_budget: VIABLE but off-pattern — inconsistent with the siblings' tiering; the synthetic 100k O(N) target is exactly what AC-072 deferred to post-1.0 ANN work; only if a hard 100k FTS gate is wanted at GA
hitl_fork.v_bound_result_set: ORTHOGONAL/SEPARATE — top-k LIMIT on the text branch would MEET (not tier) the 100k budget but changes recall semantics; out of scope for B2, needs a dedicated retrieval ADR

blockers_encountered: None. The reported Slice-40 49.9/315 @100k magnitude was not reproduced on this idle x86_64 box (21/99) — characterized as hardware/load sensitivity, not papered over.
additional_changes_made_in_scope: Temporary engine-level A/B: step-11 tokenize string in fathomdb-schema/src/lib.rs set to 'unicode61' for one ac_012@100k run, then reverted to 'porter unicode61 remove_diacritics 2'. Verified restored (git status clean except the new harness; canonical tripwire empty). NOT in the committed diff. Harness fmt+clippy-cleaned to not break future agent-verify.

scope_witness.committed_diff: findings doc + dev/DOC-INDEX.md row + SLICE6_EXPERIMENT-gated harness only
scope_witness.no_production_change: no tokenizer/migration/AC/perf_gates-budget change (verified via git diff over fathomdb-schema, migrations, dev/acceptance.md, perf_gates.rs = empty)
scope_witness.fmt_clippy: harness is cargo fmt --check clean + no clippy warnings; pre-commit hook passed

next_step_for_orchestrator: Route the recommendation + HITL fork to the human. On HITL sign-off of option (i): author Slice 7 to tier AC-012 (mint AC-076 mirroring ac_013's AC013_GATE_N=10_000 branch in perf_gates.rs; supersede the unconditional 100k assert; keep porter tokenizer). Fold the 10k-tier ac_012 assertion + eu7 recall onto the canonical x86_64 runner in the Slice-40 re-scope (B2 + B1 + Q3 together) so the gate actually runs per-push (the META gap). Then re-run Slice 40 Phase A. Reap the slice-6 worktree (/tmp/fdb-slice-6-20260607T003001Z) per orchestration.md §11. IMPORTANT for the orchestrator's board update: the Slice-40 output.json B2 root-cause ('porter stemmer + diacritics folding materially more expensive at query time') is now MEASURED-FALSE — correct the board's B2 framing to 'O(N) corpus-scaling latency; ac_012 never tiered'.
